sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 16.53 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.16)

statement.latex: Let$d(G)$denote the smallest cardinality of a generating set of the group~$G$. Suppose that$G= <A,B >$, where$A$and$B$are two$d$-generated finite groups of coprime orders. Is it true that$d(G) d+1$? See Archive~12.71 and (A.\,Lucchini, J.~Algebra, 245 (2001), 552--561). Comment of 2021: the answer is yes if$A$and$B$have prime power order (E.\,Detomi, A.\,Lucchini, , 87, no.\,3 (2013), 689--706). {...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
